name: Claude Mythos 5
tagline: Anthropic's Mythos-class frontier model with safety classifiers removed — limited to Project Glasswing cyberdefense partners.
metaDescription: Claude Mythos 5 is Anthropic's Mythos-class frontier model (June 2026) — the unclassifier'd twin of Fable 5, 1M context, 128K output, offered only via Project…
architecture: Proprietary Mythos-class model. Shares the same underlying model as Claude Fable 5, with Fable's blocking safety classifiers removed. Adaptive thinking is always on (extended/disabled thinking modes are not supported); thinking depth is set with the effort parameter and raw chain-of-thought is never returned.
overview: Claude Mythos 5 is Anthropic's top-tier, Mythos-class frontier model, announced on June 9, 2026 alongside its public twin, Claude Fable 5. The two share the same underlying model and the same specs and pricing; the difference is safety scope. Claude Fable 5 ships with blocking safety classifiers that decline or fall back to Claude Opus 4.8 in sensitive domains (cybersecurity, biology, chemistry, model distillation), while Claude Mythos 5 carries those classifiers removed. Because of that, Mythos 5 is not generally available — it is offered only in limited release to approved customers through Anthropic's Project Glasswing, the company's program for hardening critical software, working with the US government and partners such as AWS, Apple, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Google, Microsoft, NVIDIA and Palo Alto Networks.

Under the hood, Claude Mythos 5 carries a 1M-token context window, up to 128K output tokens per request, and a January 2026 knowledge cutoff. It accepts text and image input (including diagrams, charts and tables nested in PDFs) and returns text. It is the successor to the invitation-only Claude Mythos Preview, which Anthropic credits with finding thousands of high-severity software vulnerabilities, and Anthropic describes the Mythos-class line as the strongest cybersecurity model in the world.

Claude Mythos 5 reaches the API as model ID claude-mythos-5, billed at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens — about double Claude Opus 4.8 and roughly half the price of Mythos Preview. Days after launch, on June 12, 2026, Anthropic suspended access to both Claude Mythos 5 and Claude Fable 5 following a US export-control directive on access by foreign nationals; the model is currently unavailable pending resolution.
strengths: State-of-the-art software engineering and agentic coding — 95.0% on SWE-bench Verified (Anthropic-reported)
Long-horizon autonomy: plans across stages, delegates to sub-agents, and self-verifies, sustaining work across multi-day tasks
Same frontier capability as Fable 5 but without the blocking safety classifiers, so requests in sensitive domains are not refused or routed away
Strongest cybersecurity capability in the Claude line — built for vulnerability discovery and critical-software defense via Project Glasswing
1M-token context with vision over documents, diagrams, charts and tables embedded in PDFs
useCases: Defensive cybersecurity: finding and analyzing software vulnerabilities across large codebases (Project Glasswing partners)
Long-horizon agentic engineering — multi-stage, multi-day autonomous coding and refactoring
Complex knowledge work and data analysis with minimal human oversight
Vision-grounded analysis of technical documents, scientific figures and PDFs
High-autonomy research workflows for approved infrastructure and government partners

pricing.note: Shares Fable 5's pricing. 5-minute cache write $12.50/MTok; 1-hour cache write $20/MTok. Batch API: $5/MTok input, $25/MTok output. Limited availability via Project Glasswing.

faq.a: Claude Mythos 5 is Anthropic's top-tier, Mythos-class frontier model, announced on June 9, 2026. It is the same underlying model as the public Claude Fable 5 but with Fable's blocking safety classifiers removed, and it is offered only in limited release to approved Project Glasswing partners rather than generally available.

faq.q: How is Claude Mythos 5 different from Claude Fable 5?
faq.a: They share the same model, specs and pricing. Claude Fable 5 includes safety classifiers that can decline requests or fall back to Claude Opus 4.8 in sensitive domains; Claude Mythos 5 does not include those classifiers, which is why it is restricted to vetted Project Glasswing customers instead of being publicly available.

faq.q: How much does Claude Mythos 5 cost?
faq.a: It is priced the same as Claude Fable 5: $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens, with a $1/MTok cache-hit rate. Batch API pricing is $5/MTok input and $25/MTok output. Access requires Project Glasswing approval.

faq.q: Can I use Claude Mythos 5 today?
faq.a: Not currently. On June 12, 2026 — three days after launch — Anthropic suspended access to both Claude Mythos 5 and Claude Fable 5 following a US export-control directive regarding access by foreign nationals. Even before that, Mythos 5 was never generally available; access was limited to approved Project Glasswing customers.
